problem: Find the last three digits of $9^{105}.$

classified_questions.question: Can you express $9^{105}$ in a different form using algebraic manipulation?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we use the Binomial Theorem to find the last three digits of $9^{105}$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why do we ignore the terms up to $\binom{105}{102} 10^3$ when finding the last three digits?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we simplify the expression $-\binom{105}{103} 10^2 + \binom{105}{104} 10 - 1$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is the result we obtain from simplifying the expression the last three digits of $9^{105}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of other similar problems that involve finding the last few digits of large powers?
classified_questions.label: e
